text: Data from the open scientific literature serves as the basis for the definition of a set of evaluation criteria, as well as the construction of teaching tools and data sets for validation. We present Version 4 of the Insight ToolKit (ITKv4) that brings about a leap in the image registration methodology with the aim of setting a new standard in the open source community. As compared to previous versions of ITK, several important technical advances are introduced: multi-variate images and objective functions are supported; high dimensional (deformation field) and low dimensional (affine) transforms are unified through reusable metrics defined on the infinite dimensional space of transforms, as well as through composable transforms that allow for a seamless combination of any number of geometric transforms. Additional advances include: multi-processor support for metrics and optimizers, more efficient parameter management using principled heuristics for choosing scaling factors among parameters having vastly different scales (e.g. rotations and translations), as compared to Others), and constrained step sizes for gradient-based optimization. With the advances in the ITKv4 framework, researchers can spend more time on the construction and comparison of registration algorithms. We demonstrate the new functionalities of the ITK framework through a reference evaluation study of registrations of a large data set of labeled pediatric brain images.
